questions.question: A network interface has a packet arrival rate of 100 packets per second. The network bandwidth is 10 Mbps. If the packet size is 1000 bytes, calculate the packet loss percentage if the network is not able to send all packets. Assume that the network is unable to send packets at a rate faster than the packet arrival rate.
questions.hint: Use the bandwidth to calculate the packet loss percentage

questions.question: A router is configured to drop packets with a TTL (Time To Live) of 20. A packet is sent from host A to host B with a TTL of 30. What is the number of packets that will be dropped by the router?
questions.hint: Consider the packet's TTL and the router's packet drop policy

questions.question: A network switch is configured to drop packets with a size greater than 1500 bytes. A packet with a size of 1200 bytes is sent to the switch. What is the number of packets that will be dropped by the switch?
questions.hint: Compare the packet size with the threshold size
